bridgeName: full-Phi source noncollapse to global Weyl/Volterra Schur

auditItems.label: Riemann kernel formula and harmless xi normalization
auditItems.reason: The draft states the positive-side Phi formula, its even extension, and the xi Fourier representation up to an overall conventional scalar.  Such a scalar does not affect kernel positivity.

auditItems.label: KLM convention equals Weyl positivity for hbar=1
auditItems.reason: The notes and draft use the same hbar=1 phase convention.  This link is the standard KLM/quantum-Bochner equivalence in the chosen Weyl normalization.

auditItems.label: phase-space symbol transported to coordinate Weyl kernel
auditItems.reason: The draft displays the Weyl symbol sigma_omega and the associated coordinate kernel K_omega.  This closes the algebraic symbol-to-kernel transport inside the stated convention.

auditItems.label: full-line Weyl kernel reduced to even/odd half-line sectors
auditItems.reason: The evenness K(a,b)=K(-a,-b) and the even/odd half-line kernels are explicitly recorded.  Positivity of the full-line operator is equivalent to positivity of both parity sectors.

auditItems.label: normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra quotient Schur certificate
auditItems.reason: Imported quotient and bridge ledgers close positivity on ker R_global, the Douglas cross-form condition, the bounded trace-side repair, and the Moore-Penrose Schur form.

auditItems.label: full-Phi source tail and continuum high-block passage
auditItems.reason: The full-theta source quadrature, synchronized active range, and source-inactive high-block domination are already imported by the quotient Schur theorem.

auditItems.label: omega coverage for the original target |omega|<1/2
auditItems.reason: The uniform omega bridge proves that the final Hardy/Green contraction is omega-independent: kappa=(1-s-u)/(1+s+u) does not depend on omega, while the branch factors are positive and uniformly integrable for |omega|<1/2.

auditItems.label: repair-free primitive lift / D_q=0 theorem
auditItems.reason: The quotient-to-original Weyl lift ledger closes this link.

auditItems.label: original Weyl kernel positivity for all test sets
auditItems.reason: Closed by the uniform omega bridge: the quotient-to-original lift is closed, primitive endpoint correction vanishes, and the omega-independent Green-lift contraction gives positivity for every |omega|<1/2.

auditItems.label: KLM quantum positive-type condition for the original Q_omega
auditItems.reason: Closed by the hbar=1 KLM/Weyl equivalence together with uniform original Weyl positivity.

auditItems.label: implication from this KLM/Weyl positivity to the RH/de Branges target
auditItems.reason: Closed by the augmented KLM-to-de Branges closed-cone bridge and the shifted-Xi endpoint passage.  The endpoint argument uses the diagonal de Branges inequality for every 0<omega<1/2 to exclude upper-half-plane Xi zeros by a zero-descent accumulation contradiction, then uses conjugation to exclude lower-half-plane zeros.

externalEquivalenceAudit.interpretation: The normalized full-Phi quotient Schur certificate is closed, and the basic Weyl/KLM convention and kernel algebra are documented.  The quotient-to-original lift, uniform omega coverage, original Weyl positivity, and KLM positive-type packaging are closed.  The remaining external gap is the final de Branges/RH bridge theorem.

endpointIntervalKrawczykCertificateStatu.label: synchronized endpoint interval/Krawczyk certificate
endpointIntervalKrawczykCertificateStatu.reason: The synchronized coefficient certificate encloses the scaled companion and active boundary rows inside the persistent Pluecker chart.  The reported Krawczyk and chart ratios are all strictly below one.

endpointFullActiveRowRankStatus.label: endpoint map has full active row rank
endpointFullActiveRowRankStatus.reason: Nonvanishing of the persistent Pluecker chart coordinate gives full row rank for the active endpoint map.

endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.label: endpoint Green BVP solvable for every active row
endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.reason: Full active endpoint row rank removes Fredholm compatibility obstructions, so the endpoint free vector can be chosen for every active source row.

greenTraceRepresentationStatus.label: active Green trace representation
greenTraceRepresentationStatus.reason: The Volterra/Sturm Lagrange identity gives P_active E f as an interval trace integral once the endpoint BVP is solved; the endpoint concomitant is absorbed by the chosen free vector.

closedTraceActiveAnnihilationStatus.label: closed trace annihilates active source
closedTraceActiveAnnihilationStatus.reason: If R_global f=0, then every interval trace Lambda_a(f) vanishes.  The Green trace representation therefore gives E_active f=0.

activeRangeInclusionStatus.label: E_active in closure Range(R_global^*)
activeRangeInclusionStatus.reason: For the closed trace operator R_global, closure Range(R_global^*)=(ker R_global)^perp.  The closed-trace annihilation implication places each active source row in that closed range.

sourceInactiveTailDominationStatus.label: source-inactive min-max tail domination
sourceInactiveTailDominationStatus.reason: The imported min-max theorem bounds the source-inactive component by epsilon_delta in the normalized source metric, and epsilon_delta is below the low/mid Schur budget.

certifiedModelCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: active range plus inactive tail in the certified source model
certifiedModelCombinedSourceBoundStatus.reason: On ker R_global the active component vanishes; the inactive component is bounded by epsilon_delta and absorbed with positive Schur slack.

fullContinuumCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: full continuum active range plus inactive tail
fullContinuumCombinedSourceBoundStatus.reason: The active range theorem is closed by the endpoint interval certificate, and the inactive-tail theorem imports the high-block exhaustion passage.

endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.label: endpoint Green BVP solvable for every active row

certifiedModelCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: active range plus inactive tail in the certified source model

fullContinuumCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: full continuum active range plus inactive tail

formalProof: The endpoint interval/Krawczyk certificate encloses the exact active endpoint flow in a Pluecker chart whose persistent coordinate remains separated from zero.
Therefore the active endpoint map has full row rank.  The adjoint endpoint Green BVP is solvable for every active source row, because the Fredholm compatibility cokernel is trivial.
Solving the endpoint BVP in the Lagrange identity yields P_active E f = integral_I K(a)Lambda_a(f) da for the closed trace domain.
If R_global f=0, then Lambda_a(f)=0 on I and the integral vanishes.  Hence ker R_global is contained in ker E_active.
The Hilbert annihilator identity for closed densely defined operators gives closure Range(R_global^*)=(ker R_global)^perp, so E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*).

activeComponentRangeStatus.label: active component factors through trace range
activeComponentRangeStatus.reason: The synchronized endpoint interval/Green theorem gives R_global f=0 => E_active f=0.  By the Hilbert annihilator criterion, E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), so the active source rows are trace-side rows.

inactiveComponentDominationStatus.label: source-inactive component is A-bounded and absorbed
inactiveComponentDominationStatus.reason: The full-continuum high-block theorem gives ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> on H_M cap ker R_global, and epsilon_delta is below the finite low/mid Schur budget.

positivityOnKerRStatus.label: Schur positivity on ker R_global
positivityOnKerRStatus.reason: On ker R_global the active source part vanishes.  The inactive part is bounded by epsilon_delta and absorbed by the positive low/mid Volterra-Schur block, leaving positive slack.

douglasCrossFormStatus.label: Douglas/Moore-Penrose cross-form factorization
douglasCrossFormStatus.reason: The active cross rows are in closure Range(R_global^*).  The source-inactive residual is A-bounded; by polarization/Cauchy-Schwarz it defines a bounded Gamma into the A-completion.  This is exactly the continuum Douglas condition for the cross form.

traceSideRepairStatus.label: bounded trace-side repair operator S
traceSideRepairStatus.reason: Use the transported trace norm on X_R=R(U).  Then R_U:U->X_R is unitary, so M=(Gamma^*Gamma-C)_+ defines a bounded S(Ru)=M^(1/2)u without requiring closed range in ambient L2(I).

quotientFactorizationStatus.label: Weyl/Volterra quotient factorization
quotientFactorizationStatus.reason: With a>=0 on ker R_global and the bounded Douglas factor Gamma, the abstract quotient theorem gives Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2.

moorePenroseSchurStatus.label: Moore-Penrose Schur-form hypotheses
moorePenroseSchurStatus.reason: The factorization supplies the singular range condition and the nonnegative Moore-Penrose Schur form in the quotient sense.

normalizedFullPhiSchurCertificateStatus.label: normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra Schur certificate
normalizedFullPhiSchurCertificateStatus.reason: This closes the Schur certificate for the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model represented by the imported active-range, source-quadrature, and source-inactive high-block certificates.

globalWeylVolterraSchurStatus.label: normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra Schur certificate
globalWeylVolterraSchurStatus.reason: This closes the Schur certificate for the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model represented by the imported active-range, source-quadrature, and source-inactive high-block certificates.

proofChain.statement: Closed active range: E_active in closure Range(R_global^*).
Closed inactive domination: ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> on the full closed high block.
On ker R_global, active rows vanish and inactive rows are absorbed by the finite low/mid Schur budget; hence a>=0.
Active trace factorization plus inactive A-boundedness gives the Douglas cross-form condition.
The continuum quotient theorem yields Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2 and the singular Schur/range hypotheses.

quotientSchurAssembly.formalConclusion: For the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model, the two continuum Schur hypotheses are verified: Q_Phi is nonnegative on ker R_global, and the cross block has the bounded Douglas/Moore-Penrose factorization.  Therefore Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2 with S bounded on the transported trace range, and Q_Phi(f)>=0 whenever R_global f=0.
quotientSchurAssembly.externalCaveat: This closes the Weyl/Volterra quotient Schur certificate inside the current normalized full-Phi framework.  It does not by itself certify every external equivalence from this Schur certificate to the Riemann hypothesis.

remainingAnalyticItems.name: closed active range inclusion
remainingAnalyticItems.statement: E_active in closure Range(R_global^*) for the continuum active source plane, not only the sampled Galerkin range.
remainingAnalyticItems.analyticStatus: closed by synchronized endpoint interval/Krawczyk/Green BVP theorem

remainingAnalyticItems.name: source-inactive high-frequency Schur tail
remainingAnalyticItems.statement: ||(I-P_delta)E_Phi f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f>, with epsilon_delta absorbed by the finite low/mid Schur block.

finiteDiagnostic.interpretation: Finite source-inactive/high-frequency tail diagnostic only.  This is not yet the continuum Schur tail theorem.

fullPhiInactiveCertificate.interpretation: Finite full-Phi source-inactive Schur-tail certificate.  The continuum inactive bound uses lambda_3(S_{8,h}) plus the certified full-Phi source quadrature/tail error.  The budget comparison is a finite diagnostic; the continuum high-frequency Hardy/Schur tail theorem remains the analytic target.

absorptionCertificate.conditionalTheorem: If the continuum high-frequency Hardy/Schur estimate ||(I-P_delta)E_Phi f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> is proved on H_M cap ker R_global with epsilon_delta <= epsilonDeltaUpper, then the finite low/mid Schur block absorbs this source-inactive tail because epsilonDeltaUpper < finiteLowMidSchurBudget.
absorptionCertificate.remainingAnalyticGap: Prove the continuum Hardy/Schur estimate itself, not just the finite spectral inactive bound.  The numerical absorption margin is positive once that theorem supplies the stated epsilon_delta.

proof: Let S=E_Phi^*E_Phi on the A-Hilbert space.  If P_2 is the spectral projection onto the two largest eigenvalues of S, then <S(I-P_2)f,(I-P_2)f> <= lambda_3(S)<A f,f>.
The certified finite source model has lambda_3(S_{8,h})=1.138043366571e+04 and ||S-S_{8,h}||<=1.882559596904e+08; Weyl monotonicity gives lambda_3(S)<=1.882673401241e+08.
The same perturbation bound gives the top source scale lambda_1(S)>=1.399286268192e+11.  After normalizing E_Phi by sqrt(sourceTopLower), the tail constant is 1.345452638275e-03.
Since this is below the finite low/mid Schur budget 5.730309711104e-03, the normalized source-inactive tail is absorbed with slack 4.384857072828e-03.

compactKernelEllipticityStatus.label: compact commuted-kernel Sobolev ellipticity
compactKernelEllipticityStatus.reason: False as a continuum theorem.  Compactly supported oscillatory packets in ker R make the compact commuted-kernel form vanish relative to W^{m,2}; the notes and obstruction certificate rule out proving coercivity from this object alone.

finiteCommutedDominationStatus.label: finite commuted domination
finiteCommutedDominationStatus.reason: Finite Galerkin evidence shows subunit domination for suitable cutoff/order pairs, but this uses the compact commuted matrix and does not itself prove the continuum trace theorem.

sourceRangeHardyGreenStatus.label: continuum source-range Hardy/Green estimate
sourceRangeHardyGreenStatus.reason: The hardened source-range theorem imports the completed Green-lift contraction and active-range interval theorem, giving A-bounded source rows and uniform A-Riesz representers without using scan-backed representer evidence.

rieszRepresenterEquivalenceStatus.label: A-Riesz representer equivalence
rieszRepresenterEquivalenceStatus.reason: In a Hilbert space, E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A is equivalent to the scalar source rows being A-bounded; the optimal eta_u is the top eigenvalue of the Gram matrix of their A-Riesz representers.

finiteGreenRepresenterStatus.label: finite Galerkin Green representers
finiteGreenRepresenterStatus.reason: Finite closed-trace high-block representers solve the A-Riesz range equations to roundoff in the current Galerkin model.

sourceWindowDerivativeCoverStatus.label: finite source-window derivative covering
sourceWindowDerivativeCoverStatus.reason: The analytic u-derivative source rows give a finite-net covering bound over the source window; this is evidence for uniformity, not the continuum representer construction itself.

continuumGreenRepresenterStatus.label: continuum closed-trace Green representers
continuumGreenRepresenterStatus.reason: The hardened source-range theorem imports the completed Green-lift contraction and active-range interval theorem, giving A-bounded source rows and uniform A-Riesz representers without using scan-backed representer evidence.

hardenedSourceRangeTheorem.proofClass: analytic proof with interval/ball certificate inputs

activeTraceControlStatus.label: active source rows factor through closed trace
activeTraceControlStatus.reason: The synchronized active-range interval theorem and the hardened closed-trace unique-continuation theorem give R_global f=0 => E_active f=0.  Thus active source rows lie in closure Ran(R_global^*) and vanish on H_M cap ker R_global.

greenSourceControlStatus.label: Green source rows are A-bounded
greenSourceControlStatus.reason: The closed Green-lift theorem passes the Volterra/Sturm integration-by-parts identity to the completed trace-fiber domain and gives the compressed Hardy contraction.  With the active endpoint obstruction removed, the source row is bounded by the A-energy.

closedTraceHardyGreenEstimateStatus.label: closed-trace Hardy/Green estimate
closedTraceHardyGreenEstimateStatus.reason: On H_M cap ker R_global the active endpoint component vanishes.  The closed Green-lift form-domain theorem controls the remaining Green source row, so E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A.

uniformRieszRepresenterStatus.label: uniform A-Riesz Green representers
uniformRieszRepresenterStatus.reason: Each scalar source row is A-bounded on the A-Hilbert high block, so the Riesz theorem gives g_{u,k}.  The source rows and Green coefficients depend continuously on u, and the source window [0.08,0.52] is compact, giving a uniform A-norm bound for the 2x2 representer Gram.

globalTraceSourceObservabilityStatus.label: global trace/source observability on closed high block
globalTraceSourceObservabilityStatus.reason: If f lies in ker R_global cap H_M, active source rows are annihilated and the remaining source-inactive rows are controlled by the A-energy.  This is the continuum replacement for the sampled global trace/source observability scan.

compactSourceOperatorStatus.label: compact A-bounded source operator
compactSourceOperatorStatus.reason: The map u -> (g_{u,1},g_{u,2}) is continuous from the compact source interval into the A-Hilbert space.  Bochner/Riemann sums approximate E^*E by finite-rank operators in norm, so E^*E is compact and A-bounded on the closed high block.

formalProof: Let H_hi be H_M cap ker R_global with inner product <f,g>_A=<Af,g>.  For fixed u, write E_u f=(ell_1(f),ell_2(f)).
If ell_k(f)=<g_k,f>_A for g_k in H_hi, then ||E_u f||^2 <= lambda_max((<g_i,g_j>_A)) ||f||_A^2 by Cauchy-Schwarz in the finite-dimensional representer span.
Conversely, E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A makes each ell_k A-bounded, so the Riesz theorem supplies the representers.  Thus the estimate and Green representer theorem are equivalent.
Uniform source-window control follows if u -> g_{u,k} is continuous in H_A and the representer Gram is bounded on the compact source window.
sourceRangeHardyGreenTheorem.remainingAnalyticGap: The hardened source-range theorem imports the completed Green-lift contraction and active-range interval theorem, giving A-bounded source rows and uniform A-Riesz representers without using scan-backed representer evidence.
sourceRangeHardyGreenTheorem.interpretation: The source-range Hardy/Green theorem is closed by the hardened continuum Green-lift/active-range theorem.  Finite representer and source-window scans remain useful diagnostics, but are not proof dependencies of this ledger.

compactSourceExhaustionStatus.label: compact-source high-block exhaustion implication
compactSourceExhaustionStatus.reason: The corrected high-block proof is a compact-source/Mosco theorem, not a raw compact-kernel Sobolev coercivity theorem.  It closes the functional-analytic implication conditional on the continuum trace-frame lower bound.

activeSourceInputStatus.label: active source annihilation/range input
activeSourceInputStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_interval_theorem.py: R_global f=0 implies P_active Ehat f=0, equivalently E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*).

certifiedSourceModelStatus.label: certified normalized source model input
certifiedSourceModelStatus.reason: The finite/certified full-theta source model gives the normalized inactive-tail constant and verifies it is below the low/mid Schur budget.

abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.label: abstract Mosco limsup with bounded trace correction
abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.reason: If V_N is A-graph dense, sampled traces converge on V_N, and the sampled trace map has a uniformly bounded right inverse, then each f in H_M has corrected approximants f_N in H_{M,N}.  The correction norm is bounded by gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N|| and tends to zero.

abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.label: abstract Mosco liminf from weak compactness and trace consistency
abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.reason: If f_N is A-bounded in H_{M,N}, Hilbert weak compactness gives a subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to R_global f=0, and finite low/mid A-orthogonality passes by strong convergence of the removed modes.

compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.label: compact source operator norm convergence
compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.reason: Mosco convergence of closed subspaces is equivalent to strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections.  For compact S=Ehat^*Ehat, strong projection convergence gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.label: continuum min-max passage
minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.reason: Norm convergence of compact positive source operators implies convergence of lambda_3 and of the spectral min-max tail.  The continuum P_2 estimate follows from Courant-Fischer.

finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.label: finite trace-frame evidence
finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.reason: The finite weighted trace frames have positive observed lower bound and stable trace-mesh refinement.  This is numerical evidence for, not a standalone analytic proof of, the continuum uniform frame lower bound.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.label: full-Phi active source noncollapse
sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.reason: Imported from the full-theta source noncollapse interval theorem.  It proves that E_active is injective on the active source spectral space after interval source quadrature, theta-tail propagation, and Riesz projector stability.

activeRangeStatus.label: closed active range implication
activeRangeStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_interval_theorem.py: E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), equivalently R_global f=0 implies E_active f=0.

traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.label: R_global injective on H_delta
traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.reason: If R_global f=0 on H_delta, active range gives E_active f=0.  Source-side noncollapse makes E_active injective on H_delta, so f=0.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum L2 trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: The A-unit sphere in finite-dimensional H_delta is compact, and f -> ||Lambda_.(f)||_{L2(I)} is continuous and nonzero there by trace injectivity.  Its minimum gamma_delta is therefore positive.

traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.label: interval trace quadrature consistency on H_delta
traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.reason: The imported interval quadrature theorem bounds ||Gamma-Gamma_h|| by the composite trapezoid error formula, and also bounds the error relatively in the certified finite frame metric.

boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.label: bounded sampled-trace correction right inverse on active block
boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.reason: For sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete frame lower bound is at least gamma_delta/2.  The Moore-Penrose right inverse of W^{1/2}R_N on the active trace block then has norm at most sqrt(2/gamma_delta).

explicitNumericGammaStatus.label: explicit rigorous numeric gamma_delta
explicitNumericGammaStatus.reason: The finite interval trace-frame certificate gives gamma_N^->0.  The trace quadrature interval theorem controls Gamma-Gamma_h relatively in the finite frame metric with relative error below one.  Therefore the continuum trace frame has gamma_delta >= 8.291376250306e+01.

proof: Let H_delta be the finite-dimensional active source spectral space for the compact source operator S=Ehat^*Ehat.
Suppose f in H_delta and Lambda_a(f)=0 for every a in I.  Then R_global f=0, so the synchronized active range interval theorem gives E_active f=0.
The full-Phi source-side noncollapse theorem says E_active is injective on H_delta.  Hence f=0.  Therefore the continuum trace map is injective on H_delta.
The function q(f)=int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da is continuous on the compact A-unit sphere of H_delta and is strictly positive there.  Thus gamma_delta=min q(f)>0.

Because H_delta is finite dimensional and a -> Lambda_a(f) is continuous, positive quadrature rules converge uniformly on the compact family q_f(a)=|Lambda_a(f)|^2.  The discrete weighted frame matrices converge to the continuum frame matrix.
For all sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete smallest frame eigenvalue is at least gamma_delta/2.  The sampled trace correction right inverse is therefore bounded by sqrt(2/gamma_delta).
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundTheorem.formalConclusion: There exists an explicit certified lower bound gamma_delta >= 8.291376250306e+01 such that int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da >= gamma_delta ||f||_A^2 for every f in H_delta.  The sampled trace correction right inverse is therefore bounded on the active finite-dimensional trace block.

formalProof: Let H_A be the A-Hilbert completion, R=R_global, H_M=ker(R) cap L_M^{perp_A}, and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker(R_N) cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.

Limsup: choose v_N in V_N with v_N->f in A graph norm.  Since Rf=0 and sampled traces are consistent, R_N v_N->0.  A uniform trace-frame lower bound gamma_0 supplies a correction w_N with R_N w_N=R_N v_N and ||w_N||_A<=gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N||.  Then f_N=v_N-w_N lies in ker R_N and f_N->f; finite low/mid orthogonality is corrected by the convergent finite Gram matrix.

Liminf: an A-bounded sequence f_N in H_{M,N} has a weakly convergent subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to Rf=0, and low/mid orthogonality passes to the limit, so the weak limit belongs to H_M.

Mosco convergence gives strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections Pi_N to Pi.  Since the source operator is compact after active range annihilation plus the inactive Hardy/Schur bound, ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S|| tends to zero.

Therefore lambda_3(S_N)->lambda_3(S).  The finite normalized inactive-tail bound passes to H_M, giving the same epsilon constant in the full closed trace space once the continuum trace-frame lower bound is supplied.
compactSourceExhaustionProof.conditionalConclusion: Assuming the continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound/quadrature consistency, the certified normalized estimate ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= 1.345452638275e-03<A f,f> passes to H_M cap ker R_global and is absorbed by the low/mid budget 5.730309711104e-03 with slack 4.384857072828e-03.

endpointTraceBoundednessStatus.label: endpoint source-row trace boundedness
endpointTraceBoundednessStatus.reason: Follows once the compact-source exhaustion theorem is unconditional; the current remaining input is the continuum trace-frame lower bound for sampled trace correction.

compactObstruction.interpretation: For the compact kernel model S_m^comm, smooth oscillatory packets supported away from the endpoint trace interval drive S_m/W_m toward zero. The continuum elliptic estimate therefore needs the local positive terms from the true commuted Sturm identity.

sourceRangeCertificate.interpretation: Full Sobolev W10 controls the residual but is not absorbable by the compact positive block. The finite source-range regularizer is the viable auxiliary object; its absorption constant is the direct high-block Schur/Douglas constant.

ellipticTraceTheorem.correctedTheorem: For the closed continuum trace space and after removing the finite low/mid A-modes, prove E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A uniformly in u on the source window.  This source-range Hardy/Green estimate is the elliptic trace input needed by the high-block exhaustion theorem.
ellipticTraceTheorem.invalidRoute: Do not try to prove raw local a_10(s)>0 Sobolev ellipticity for the compact endpoint commuted-kernel form; the compact packet obstruction disproves that route.
formalConsequencesIfClosed: Endpoint source rows are A-bounded on H_M cap ker R_global.
The normalized source operator Ehat_Phi is compact/finite-rank in the A-Hilbert source model.
P_N Ehat^*Ehat P_N -> Ehat^*Ehat follows once A-graph Galerkin/Mosco convergence is also proved.
The high-block exhaustion ledger can then close sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus after Mosco is supplied.
ellipticTraceTheorem.interpretation: The corrected elliptic trace target has been sharpened: the compact-kernel Sobolev route is invalid, while the compact-source exhaustion implication is proved conditionally.  The remaining non-formal input is the continuum trace-frame lower bound.

activeSourceInputStatus.label: active source annihilation/range input
activeSourceInputStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_interval_theorem.py: R_global f=0 implies P_active Ehat f=0, equivalently E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*).

certifiedSourceModelStatus.label: certified normalized source model input
certifiedSourceModelStatus.reason: The finite/certified full-theta source model gives the normalized inactive-tail constant and verifies it is below the low/mid Schur budget.

abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.label: abstract Mosco limsup with bounded trace correction
abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.reason: If V_N is A-graph dense, sampled traces converge on V_N, and the sampled trace map has a uniformly bounded right inverse, then each f in H_M has corrected approximants f_N in H_{M,N}.  The correction norm is bounded by gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N|| and tends to zero.

abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.label: abstract Mosco liminf from weak compactness and trace consistency
abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.reason: If f_N is A-bounded in H_{M,N}, Hilbert weak compactness gives a subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to R_global f=0, and finite low/mid A-orthogonality passes by strong convergence of the removed modes.

compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.label: compact source operator norm convergence
compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.reason: Mosco convergence of closed subspaces is equivalent to strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections.  For compact S=Ehat^*Ehat, strong projection convergence gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.label: continuum min-max passage
minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.reason: Norm convergence of compact positive source operators implies convergence of lambda_3 and of the spectral min-max tail.  The continuum P_2 estimate follows from Courant-Fischer.

finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.label: finite trace-frame evidence
finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.reason: The finite weighted trace frames have positive observed lower bound and stable trace-mesh refinement.  This is numerical evidence for, not a standalone analytic proof of, the continuum uniform frame lower bound.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.label: full-Phi active source noncollapse
sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.reason: Imported from the full-theta source noncollapse interval theorem.  It proves that E_active is injective on the active source spectral space after interval source quadrature, theta-tail propagation, and Riesz projector stability.

activeRangeStatus.label: closed active range implication
activeRangeStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_interval_theorem.py: E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), equivalently R_global f=0 implies E_active f=0.

traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.label: R_global injective on H_delta
traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.reason: If R_global f=0 on H_delta, active range gives E_active f=0.  Source-side noncollapse makes E_active injective on H_delta, so f=0.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum L2 trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: The A-unit sphere in finite-dimensional H_delta is compact, and f -> ||Lambda_.(f)||_{L2(I)} is continuous and nonzero there by trace injectivity.  Its minimum gamma_delta is therefore positive.

traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.label: interval trace quadrature consistency on H_delta
traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.reason: The imported interval quadrature theorem bounds ||Gamma-Gamma_h|| by the composite trapezoid error formula, and also bounds the error relatively in the certified finite frame metric.

boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.label: bounded sampled-trace correction right inverse on active block
boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.reason: For sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete frame lower bound is at least gamma_delta/2.  The Moore-Penrose right inverse of W^{1/2}R_N on the active trace block then has norm at most sqrt(2/gamma_delta).

explicitNumericGammaStatus.label: explicit rigorous numeric gamma_delta
explicitNumericGammaStatus.reason: The finite interval trace-frame certificate gives gamma_N^->0.  The trace quadrature interval theorem controls Gamma-Gamma_h relatively in the finite frame metric with relative error below one.  Therefore the continuum trace frame has gamma_delta >= 8.291376250306e+01.

proof: Let H_delta be the finite-dimensional active source spectral space for the compact source operator S=Ehat^*Ehat.
Suppose f in H_delta and Lambda_a(f)=0 for every a in I.  Then R_global f=0, so the synchronized active range interval theorem gives E_active f=0.
The full-Phi source-side noncollapse theorem says E_active is injective on H_delta.  Hence f=0.  Therefore the continuum trace map is injective on H_delta.
The function q(f)=int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da is continuous on the compact A-unit sphere of H_delta and is strictly positive there.  Thus gamma_delta=min q(f)>0.

Because H_delta is finite dimensional and a -> Lambda_a(f) is continuous, positive quadrature rules converge uniformly on the compact family q_f(a)=|Lambda_a(f)|^2.  The discrete weighted frame matrices converge to the continuum frame matrix.
For all sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete smallest frame eigenvalue is at least gamma_delta/2.  The sampled trace correction right inverse is therefore bounded by sqrt(2/gamma_delta).
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundTheorem.formalConclusion: There exists an explicit certified lower bound gamma_delta >= 8.291376250306e+01 such that int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da >= gamma_delta ||f||_A^2 for every f in H_delta.  The sampled trace correction right inverse is therefore bounded on the active finite-dimensional trace block.

formalProof: Let H_A be the A-Hilbert completion, R=R_global, H_M=ker(R) cap L_M^{perp_A}, and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker(R_N) cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.

Limsup: choose v_N in V_N with v_N->f in A graph norm.  Since Rf=0 and sampled traces are consistent, R_N v_N->0.  A uniform trace-frame lower bound gamma_0 supplies a correction w_N with R_N w_N=R_N v_N and ||w_N||_A<=gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N||.  Then f_N=v_N-w_N lies in ker R_N and f_N->f; finite low/mid orthogonality is corrected by the convergent finite Gram matrix.

Liminf: an A-bounded sequence f_N in H_{M,N} has a weakly convergent subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to Rf=0, and low/mid orthogonality passes to the limit, so the weak limit belongs to H_M.

Mosco convergence gives strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections Pi_N to Pi.  Since the source operator is compact after active range annihilation plus the inactive Hardy/Schur bound, ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S|| tends to zero.

Therefore lambda_3(S_N)->lambda_3(S).  The finite normalized inactive-tail bound passes to H_M, giving the same epsilon constant in the full closed trace space once the continuum trace-frame lower bound is supplied.
compactSourceExhaustionProof.conditionalConclusion: Assuming the continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound/quadrature consistency, the certified normalized estimate ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= 1.345452638275e-03<A f,f> passes to H_M cap ker R_global and is absorbed by the low/mid budget 5.730309711104e-03 with slack 4.384857072828e-03.

abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.label: abstract compact-source exhaustion theorem
abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.reason: The Hilbert-space Mosco/compact-source/min-max implication is closed.  It is conditional only on the continuum trace-frame lower bound needed to identify the sampled closed-trace Galerkin spaces with H_M.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup/recovery sequence
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the recovery sequence once the sampled trace map has a continuum uniform lower frame bound and bounded correction right inverse.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf/compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the weak compactness/trace-closure argument once sampled trace quadrature is known to converge on A-bounded high-block sequences.

ellipticTraceStatus.label: commuted Sturm/Volterra elliptic estimate
ellipticTraceStatus.reason: The compact-source exhaustion proof supplies the valid replacement for the invalid raw compact-kernel Sobolev route.

sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.label: source operator norm convergence
sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.reason: The compact-source proof shows that Mosco convergence of the closed high blocks plus compactness of S=Ehat^*Ehat gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.label: Riesz spectral projection convergence
spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.reason: Standard consequence of ||P_N S P_N-S||_{A->A}->0 and an open gap around the top-two source spectrum.

minmaxPassageStatus.label: min-max inequality passage
minmaxPassageStatus.reason: Once source operator norm convergence and spectral projection convergence hold, the Courant-Fischer/Riesz projection argument passes the normalized tail inequality to the continuum.

formalProof: Define H_A as the Hilbert completion under the positive Volterra/Sturm form A, N=ker R_global, and H_M=N cap L_M^{perp_A}.
Let V_N be the polynomial Galerkin space and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker R_N cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.  Mosco convergence H_{M,N}->H_M is the required exhaustion theorem.

The source row is the direct Lagrange/adjoint pair E_u f=(B_P[h_u,f](s0), (P^*h_u)(s0)f(s0)).  The source-range Hardy/Green theorem constructs its A-Riesz representers uniformly, so S=E^*E is compact in the A-Hilbert source model.
A-graph Galerkin convergence plus compact direct source-row factorization gives ||P_NSP_N-S||_{A->A}->0.
Riesz projection convergence then gives P_{2,N}->P_2, and the normalized finite min-max tail inequality passes to H_M cap ker R_global by recovery sequences and lower limits.
highBlockExhaustionTheorem.interpretation: The functional-analytic compact-source passage is proved, and the continuum trace-frame lower-bound theorem supplies the missing bounded correction right inverse.  The normalized tail estimate now passes to the full closed high block.

remainingAnalyticItems.analyticStatus: closed in the full continuum high block

remainingAnalyticItems.name: closed-trace quotient factorization
remainingAnalyticItems.statement: Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2 with S bounded on the completed trace range; equivalently positivity on ker R_global plus the Douglas cross-form condition.

activeComponentRangeStatus.label: active component factors through trace range
activeComponentRangeStatus.reason: The synchronized endpoint interval/Green theorem gives R_global f=0 => E_active f=0.  By the Hilbert annihilator criterion, E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), so the active source rows are trace-side rows.

inactiveComponentDominationStatus.label: source-inactive component is A-bounded and absorbed
inactiveComponentDominationStatus.reason: The full-continuum high-block theorem gives ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> on H_M cap ker R_global, and epsilon_delta is below the finite low/mid Schur budget.

positivityOnKerRStatus.label: Schur positivity on ker R_global
positivityOnKerRStatus.reason: On ker R_global the active source part vanishes.  The inactive part is bounded by epsilon_delta and absorbed by the positive low/mid Volterra-Schur block, leaving positive slack.

douglasCrossFormStatus.label: Douglas/Moore-Penrose cross-form factorization
douglasCrossFormStatus.reason: The active cross rows are in closure Range(R_global^*).  The source-inactive residual is A-bounded; by polarization/Cauchy-Schwarz it defines a bounded Gamma into the A-completion.  This is exactly the continuum Douglas condition for the cross form.

traceSideRepairStatus.label: bounded trace-side repair operator S
traceSideRepairStatus.reason: Use the transported trace norm on X_R=R(U).  Then R_U:U->X_R is unitary, so M=(Gamma^*Gamma-C)_+ defines a bounded S(Ru)=M^(1/2)u without requiring closed range in ambient L2(I).

quotientFactorizationStatus.label: Weyl/Volterra quotient factorization
quotientFactorizationStatus.reason: With a>=0 on ker R_global and the bounded Douglas factor Gamma, the abstract quotient theorem gives Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2.

moorePenroseSchurStatus.label: Moore-Penrose Schur-form hypotheses
moorePenroseSchurStatus.reason: The factorization supplies the singular range condition and the nonnegative Moore-Penrose Schur form in the quotient sense.

normalizedFullPhiSchurCertificateStatus.label: normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra Schur certificate
normalizedFullPhiSchurCertificateStatus.reason: This closes the Schur certificate for the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model represented by the imported active-range, source-quadrature, and source-inactive high-block certificates.

globalWeylVolterraSchurStatus.label: normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra Schur certificate
globalWeylVolterraSchurStatus.reason: This closes the Schur certificate for the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model represented by the imported active-range, source-quadrature, and source-inactive high-block certificates.

proofChain.statement: Closed active range: E_active in closure Range(R_global^*).
Closed inactive domination: ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> on the full closed high block.
On ker R_global, active rows vanish and inactive rows are absorbed by the finite low/mid Schur budget; hence a>=0.
Active trace factorization plus inactive A-boundedness gives the Douglas cross-form condition.
The continuum quotient theorem yields Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2 and the singular Schur/range hypotheses.

assemblyTheorem.formalConclusion: For the normalized full-Phi source/Volterra model, the two continuum Schur hypotheses are verified: Q_Phi is nonnegative on ker R_global, and the cross block has the bounded Douglas/Moore-Penrose factorization.  Therefore Q_Phi(f)=||Gf||^2-||S R_global f||^2 with S bounded on the transported trace range, and Q_Phi(f)>=0 whenever R_global f=0.
assemblyTheorem.externalCaveat: This closes the Weyl/Volterra quotient Schur certificate inside the current normalized full-Phi framework.  It does not by itself certify every external equivalence from this Schur certificate to the Riemann hypothesis.

remainingAnalyticItems.analyticStatus: closed by the quotient Schur assembly theorem

implication: The active source/rank obstruction, active range inclusion, source-inactive high-block domination, and quotient/Douglas Schur assembly are all closed in the normalized full-Phi Weyl/Volterra model.
